page: Amazon Web Services c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs derived from 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core testing placing in the top 25 percent. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the middle 50 percent average. Memory performance is notable, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25 percent, though uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidths remain in the average tier. In terms of specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small language models, while large model prompt processing is weak, placing in the bottom 25 percent.

The c6a.8xlarge provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high-density compute profile with dedicated AMD EPYC resources, making it a viable option for compute-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. Tradeoffs include the absence of bundled local storage and GPUs, which limits its utility for heavy data-storage operations or hardware-accelerated machine learning. It is suited for workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and moderate-scale database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance and strong memory scores, while multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth remain average. The instance is highly capable at text generation for small language models but shows weak performance on large 70B models. Given its compute-to-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core and lack of local storage, the c6a.8xlarge is best suited for compute-intensive tasks such as software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations where external storage is utilized.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 dedicated vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, and 64 GB DDR4 RAM, delivering strong single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance featuring thirty-two vCPUs and sixty-four gigabytes of RAM for compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory at a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Delivers strong single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark performance
Provides 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and moderate database tasks
